J. N. Fitzpatrick of the Union
Iron Works. in reply to a query as
to the reduction of rates on pig
iron recently announced by the
Northern Pacific railway, explained
the effects of the reduction.


He stated that for some time past
pig iron has been finding its way
to this part of the country from
England and Scotland by steam and
sailing ship to Puget sound ports
and from there by rail. BY this
route they were able to effect such
q saving in freight as to be able
to undersell the Alabama foundries.
and the new rate will enable the
eastern people to benefit by the
competition and give the railway
